config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi dasar utama, karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, kepentingan masyarakat mudah dipakai sebagai alasan untuk menekan kebebasan, kritik, dan kelompok minoritas. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat masyarakat di Indonesia, keputusan yang mengatasnamakan “demi umum” sering kali justru lebih aman kalau tetap dibatasi oleh hukum dan hak asasi yang jelas. Kepentingan sosial penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan martabat dan kebebasan individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society's interests should automatically take priority over individual rights. In the United States, our history makes me wary of any argument that says the common good can override personal liberty without very clear limits, because that language has often been used to justify abuse, discrimination, and government overreach. At the same time, individual rights are not absolute in every situation, since public safety and basic civic order matter, but those exceptions should be narrow and heavily justified. To me, the default has to be that the individual is protected first, not sacrificed for a vague idea of society's needs.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu. Di Indonesia, saya melihat sendiri betapa sering alasan “demi kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang seharusnya dilakukan adalah memastikan kepentingan publik dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat, kebebasan, dan perlindungan hukum tiap orang. Hak individu memang tidak absolut, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat ketat, jelas, dan benar-benar perlu, bukan sekadar karena mayoritas merasa itu lebih nyaman. Kalau hak individu mudah disingkirkan atas nama masyarakat, yang paling rentan justru biasanya kelompok kecil dan minoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From an American perspective, that kind of language sets off alarms because it has too often been used to excuse government overreach, discrimination, or simply the preferences of the majority crushing unpopular people. I do think there are narrow exceptions for things like public safety and emergency response, but those need strict limits and real accountability. If we start from the idea that the individual can be sacrificed for society, the people with the least power usually pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan warga yang lemah, berbeda, atau kritis. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi justru harus dicapai lewat perlindungan hak individu yang kuat, bukan dengan menjadikannya korban pertama setiap kali ada tuntutan mayoritas. Saya bisa menerima pembatasan yang sangat terbatas untuk keadaan darurat atau keselamatan publik, tetapi itu harus jelas dasar hukumnya dan benar-benar proporsional. Kalau tidak, kata “masyarakat” hanya jadi pembenaran yang rapi untuk ketidakadilan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over individual rights as a general principle. From my American perspective, that idea is dangerous because "the common good" is often the phrase people use when they want to silence dissent, punish minorities, or expand state power without enough checks. I do think there are limited cases where public safety matters more in the moment, like emergencies or disease control, but those exceptions have to be narrow, temporary, and legally accountable. In practice, protecting individual rights first is usually the best way to protect society from abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
